title: pulicaria baluchistanica
intro: Pulicaria baluchistanica, also known as Bhutera or Bhuteraa, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Pakistan and Afghanistan. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic activities, which contribute to its various health benefits. The health benefits of Pulicaria baluchistanica include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential in managing diabetes and reducing oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
